features.name: bit-slice multiplication producing narrow result
features.description: Code should include a multiplication of two bit slices from the same signal, with the result width equal to one of the operand widths, causing potential truncation of the product

features.name: partial product of same-signal slices
features.description: Code should include a multiplication where both operands are bit-selected sub-ranges of the same bus signal, which can expose optimizer assumptions about signal independence

features.name: narrow multiply with truncated result
features.description: Code should include a multiply of two 4-bit values assigned to a 4-bit output, where the full product requires 8 bits and the upper bits are implicitly truncated
features.snippet:
wire [3:0] a, b;
wire [3:0] out = a * b;
